rulings.Case Title: ALVIN RATLIFF VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT PLANT PRODUCTS & SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF POSSESSED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO NAME DEFENDANT AFTER THE SLAYEN DEPOSITION WAS TAKEN UNDER A STRICT LIABILITY THEORY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ABDULLAH SHAIKH VS. EQUINOX FITNESS CLUB OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT EQUINOX PINE STREET, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE FACTS ARE UNDISPUTED THAT DEFENDANT CALLED EMERGENCY SERVICE AND DID NOT INCREASE THE DANGER TO PLAINTIFF. THE DECISIONS IN ROTOLO V SAN JOSE SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT, L.L.C. (2007) 151 CAL APP 4TH 307 AND BREAUX V GINO'S, INC. (1984) 153 CAL APP 3D 379 HOLD THAT DEFENDANT DISCHARGED ITS DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF AND MAY NOT BE HELD LIABLE FOR USING THE A.E.D. DEVICE TO ASSIST PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS KUNG VS. HONG MIN GU et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT HONG GU'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES A JOINT VENTURE CLAIM THAT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS AND THAT THE BREACH OF THE ORAL CONTRACT OCCURRED IN FEBRUARY 2007. THE COURT MUST ACCEPT THE FACTS ALLEGED AS TRUE WHEN RULING ON A DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN E LAWRENCE VS. SEAN MCKEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder By Defendant Sean Mckean In Anti-Slapp Motion Of Shawn Mcilvenna; Joinde In Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2008 LINE 13.DEFENDANTS SEAN MCKEAN AND SHAWN MCILVENNA'S Joinder By Defendant Sean Mckean In Anti-Slapp Motion Of Shawn Mcilvenna; Joinder In Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees: The anti-SLAPP motion is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to the 8th cause of action for abuse of process which is based on protected activity of seeking the TRO that is privileged under Civil Code section 47(b). The other causes of action, 1 through 8, are based on a series of acts that include the TRO and letters to plaintiff's employer that were written in connection with defendants' dispute with plaintiff, acting in his capacity as manager of the landlord's building. As such, the letters appear to be protected by the litigation privilege. There certainly was an on-going dispute and the letters were written as a part of the dispute in an apparent effort to avoid litigation. In paragraph 11 of the complaint, plaintiff alleges that "other unprivileged, false and defamatory written and verbal communications to his employer, tenants in the Apartment Complex and other people in the community" were made. In his opposition, plaintiff attaches a flier that was placed allegedly on a utility pole purportedly written by one of the defendants (Ex. 9) accusing plaintiff of being a "total criminal, a liar, and is now attempting to break into our apartment." Defendants deny publishing the flier but that does not defeat plaintiff's showing that he could establish a prima facie claim for relief. For that reason, the motion is denied as to the balance of the causes of action. Defendants' request for fees and costs in the amount of $3620 is based on their challenge to all eight causes of action. The Court awards $425.50 in fees and costs based on defendants prevailing on one of the causes of action. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN E LAWRENCE VS. SEAN MCKEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Motions Of Shawn Mcilvenna; Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16(C); As Against Plaintiff Stephen E. Lawrence
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 23, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANTS SEAN MCKEAN AND SHAWN MCILVENNA'S (Amended) Anti-Slapp Motions Of Shawn Mcilvenna; Request For Sanctions, Costs And Fees Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 425.16(C); As Against Plaintiff Stephen E. Lawrence: The anti-SLAPP motion is granted in part and denied in part. It is granted as to the 8th cause of action for abuse of process which is based on protected activity of seeking the TRO that is privileged under Civil Code section 47(b). The other causes of action, 1 through 8, are based on a series of acts that include the TRO and letters to plaintiff's employer that were written in connection with defendants' dispute with plaintiff, acting in his capacity as manager of the landlord's building. As such, the letters appear to be protected by the litigation privilege. There certainly was an on-going dispute and the letters were written as a part of the dispute in an apparent effort to avoid litigation. In paragraph 11 of the complaint, plaintiff alleges that "other unprivileged, false and defamatory written and verbal communications to his employer, tenants in the Apartment Complex and other people in the community" were made. In his opposition, plaintiff attaches a flier that was placed allegedly on a utility pole purportedly written by one of the defendants (Ex. 9) accusing plaintiff of being a "total criminal, a liar, and is now attempting to break into our apartment." Defendants deny publishing the flier but that does not defeat plaintiff's showing that he could establish a prima facie claim for relief. For that reason, the motion is denied as to the balance of the causes of action. Defendants' request for fees and costs in the amount of $3620 is based on their challenge to all eight causes of action. The Court awards $425.50 in fees and costs based on defendants prevailing on one of the causes of action. =(302/PJM/AY)
